MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT To Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint Or In The Alternative, For Summary Ajudication
Off Calendar Jun-03-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Richard A. MacBride, Attorney at Law, Tel. 415.730.6289, for plaintiffs;
David Chen, of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter, Tel. 510.457.3992, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ARTERRA OWNERS ASSOCIATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT To Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Ajudication is denied. Privette and its progeny do not require granting of this motion. Arterra did not satisfy its burden of production. "A landowner cannot effectively delegate to the contractor responsibility for the safety of its employees if it fails to disclose critical information needed to fulfill that responsibility, and therefore the landowner would be liable to the contractor's employee if the employee's injury is attributable to an undisclosed hazard. Nothing in the Privette line of cases suggests the contrary. As in Hooker and McKown, the hirer's liability in such circumstances would be derived from the hirer's rather than the contractor's negligence." (Kinsman v. Unocal Corp. (2005) 37 Cal.4th 659, 674.) In Koephich v. Kashiwa Fudosan America, Inc. (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 32, 36-37, the court explains that: " 'The duty which a possessor of land owes to others to put and maintain it in reasonably safe condition is nondelegable. If an independent contractor, no matter how carefully selected, is employed to perform it, the possessor is answerable for harm caused by the negligent failure of his contractor to put or maintain the buildings and structures in reasonably safe condition, irrespective of whether the contractor's negligence lies in his incompetence, carelessness, inattention or delay.' " The undisputed evidence demonstrates that Arterra owned the defective pedestal that caused plaintiffs' injuries. There is a triable issue whether Arterra knew or should have known of the undisclosed hazard. (Lum Depo., 130:13-21; Anderson Dec., par. 9). Order signed in open court. Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/HK)
